examples: For example, the hereditary hemochromatosis mutation protects carriers from iron-deficiency because the mutated gene allows increased efficiency of iron absorption.
The term "hemochromatosis" is used when organ dysfunction occurs.
Bronze hyperpigmentation is seen in hemochromatosis and arsenic intoxication.
Consider hereditary hemochromatosis (HH), which could be carried by as many as 1 in 200 Americans.
In the case of hemochromatosis, which can be treated cheaply and without drugs, if detected early, there are no big money studies being done.
